posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
Some notes from the pickup even up at Rhys this past Saturday. Thanks again to Kevin and the Rhys team for making it a fun event, and the excellent wines of course.

2014 Aeris Wines Etna Bianco Superiore - Italy, Sicily, Etna DOC (5/7/2018)
First release of the new Aeris project from Rhys, old vine Carricante from a vineyard on Mt. Etna, vinified and bottled in Italy. This has delicious medium depth, great acidity balanced by fabulous fruit intensity, with umami saline notes on a long finish. Has the same kind of character as a top white Burgundy, but with the more baritone Carricante profile. (93 pts.)

Tried this next to a Rhys Bearwallow Chardonnay, an interesting contrast between the two varieties, with the Chard higher toned and brighter, a bit more opulent, though the Carricante has beautiful richness and depth.

A fantastic first effort, not surprisingly from a great, established vineyard, and an experienced winemaking team.

2015 Rhys Chardonnay Bearwallow Vineyard - USA, California, North Coast, Anderson Valley (5/7/2018)
Beautiful medium richness, excellent acidity, in the perfect Chardonnay zone. (92 pts.)

2016 Rhys Alesia Pinot Noir Anderson Valley - USA, California, North Coast, Anderson Valley (5/7/2018)
Beautifully perfumed nose, very pretty darker fruit, medium body, excellent acidity with a bit of mouthwatering tartness. Declassified Bearwallow fruit, this will be a relative bargain for its quality. (91 pts.)

2015 Rhys Pinot Noir Family Farm Vineyard - USA, California, San Francisco Bay, San Mateo County (5/7/2018)
Some savory stem notes on the nose, good, slightly sweet medium cherry fruit, stem and earth notes, but more subtle and not overpowering. Has a bit of ripeness and a sweet note on the finish. A good Family Farm showing, bit less structured and stem driven than some vintages. (90 pts.)

2015 Rhys Syrah Horseshoe Vineyard - USA, California, San Francisco Bay, Santa Cruz Mountains (5/7/2018)
Medium dark, not opaque, with a light earth/leather/spice nose; beautiful darker fruit, blackberry/boysenberry, fairly tannic but ripe and supple that should resolve well with time. A wonderful Syrah with nice depth and elegance. (92 pts.)

posts.name: Andy K0b3rl
posts.cooked:
Nice notes, Alan!

I do agree that the Carricante was delicious! I thought it’s not quite as rich as you described it, but with a lovely saline note and great depth! We’ll probably pop one in the coming weeks and keep the other two for at least a couple of years

I also thought that the Family Farm was excellent. Quite dark fruit flavors and noticeable stems (not sure what’s the percentage?), but also nice depth and complexity. I thought it was much better than the Alesia, which was a bit linear and not as deep…

posts.name: Russ Williams
posts.cooked: Glad to hear the Aeris showed so well, although certainly not surprising. I can’t wait to try one. Thoughts on additional improvement with age?

posts.name: Al Osterheld
posts.cooked:
I would hold at least some of the Aeris. Drinks lovely now, but according to Jeff, previous wines from the vineyard made by Foti developed more of the salinity and a kind of waxy texture with age.

-Al

posts.name: Peter Petersen
posts.cooked:
Thanks for the notes. Looks like we have something to look forward to.
Btw I’ve noticed that K&L has those Alesia AV wines, Chard and PN. But I’m not biting at a price that is like the Rhys AV bottle prices.

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: Thanks much. Really appreciate the Aeris note.

posts.name: Al Osterheld
posts.cooked:
Note that Alesia will now be 100% Estate fruit. I think the former Rhys appellation wines will now be released under the Alesia label.

-Al

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Our notes mesh pretty closely, but you’re quite conservative on the 2015 Family Farm - for me one of their best renditions from that site. Following a bottle over several days, there’s a lot of nuance that emerges despite its unusually seductive profile.

Carricante is definitely built to age. I have been corresponding with my Italian colleague about it and apparently 10+ years is not a problem.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
William, quick notes from pickup day should probably take a back seat to spending several days with a bottle

I’m also a conservative grader. And distracted by the pizza.

posts.name: Tom Gutting
posts.cooked: Thanks for the look, Alan. I’m keenly intrigued by the Aeris. Can’t wait for it to get here.

posts.name: Markus S
posts.cooked:
Carricante is barritone now? I’ve always thought of it as a lighter-feeling wine between a cross of ocean wines and alpine, warmed by the sun.

Envy you guys who can simply drive up the hill to Rhys. Must be nice!

posts.name: Al Osterheld
posts.cooked:
Envy you guys who can simply drive up the hill to Rhys. Must be nice!

It’s an arduous drive. Well, it was the time there snow and patches of ice on the winding road.

-Al

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
Markus S:

Carricante is barritone now? I’ve always thought of it as a lighter-feeling wine between a cross of ocean wines and alpine, warmed by the sun.

Envy you guys who can simply drive up the hill to Rhys. Must be nice!

Maybe just my own perception (or communication skills). Tasting next to each other, the Chardonnay was brighter and higher toned in overall profile, while the Carricante had a “lower pitched” type of flavor. I don’t really know how else to describe it.

It is indeed nice to be able to get to places like Rhys and Ridge in under an hour on a Saturday morning (for me, from the south Bay). Doesn’t quite make up for sitting in hours and hours of traffic the rest of the week

posts.name: Steve Brickley
posts.cooked:
Al Osterheld:

Envy you guys who can simply drive up the hill to Rhys. Must be nice!

It’s an arduous drive. Well, it was the time there snow and patches of ice on the winding road.

-Al

suggested_topics.title: TN: 2004 Vieux Donjon Châteauneuf du Pape
suggested_topics.excerpt:
Opened at a restaurant. Cork in really great condition. A bit surprised how good it looked - like a new bottle. 
Medium garnet. A bit reserved at first, but really opened up after about hour and kept improving with time.…
